reviews.review_msg: Susan is an amazing prof, who lectures with clarity and has very easy-to-follow slides. Always super encouraging of students' interpretations and suggestions. One gets the sense that she fully understands what it is like for a university student to be daunted by having to make insightful literary analysis, but she totally assuages such concerns.
Always willing to meet with students and give detailed feedback on your work. The course material was interesting and the lectures were very detailed and easy to follow.

Susan is a great professor. She is professional and a fair marker; I really respect her. Her lectures are well-organized and participation is encouraged. I learned a lot in her class. If you can take a course with her, I would do it!
Eloquent, insightful, straightforward, and cares for her students.

Susan was a great professor - you can tell that she really cares about her students and the subject matter. Although at times, lectures could be somewhat dull and too straightforward. The texts chosen were very interesting and fun to read. She loves participation though - so get ready to speak up!

Dr. Brook is incredibly sweet and helpful. English lectures are usually boring but she tries her best to keep things mildly entertaining by showing related videos; she also gives solid advice on how to structure writing. The texts she chose for the course were interesting enough. I went to her office hours once and found her advice very helpful.

Eng 115 Susan Brooks is a very good professor. Her lectures are very interesting and engaging. Course material was very good and I really enjoyed the required novels. Two weeks was given to each novel study and it was plenty of time. My TA was good at engaging the students in tutorial but her marking was extremely harsh.
Susan is a really caring professor who definitely supplies her students with the tools for success. Her short story selections were very interesting and good to read.

Susan is honestly one of my favourite profs at SFU, she genuinely cares about her students & is always willing to go the extra mile to help them. She chose four books, and you are given 2 weeks to read each one, and then two short stories. You also have to maintain a reading journal for each book. Despite the amount of work, this is class is great!

Susan is a fabulous professor. She teaches dense material but finds a way to make it relevant and interesting. Expect a ton of reading and a lot of lively group discussion, attendance is mandatory. She is very helpful in office hours and really cares about her students. You will learn a lot and have a good time doing it.
